req.title: External ACH transfer (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall transfer funds to an external ACH-linked account up to $10,000 per transfer; transfers above this shall require step-up verification. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-029 defines a hard threshold of $10,000 for ACH transfers without step-up verification. BVA demands tests at $1 (minimum valid), $10,000 (upper boundary inclusive), and $10,001 (first invalid value). The boundary kind is reserved for BVA and DT tests that probe threshold edges. The violation in test[2] was that kind=negative was paired with heuristic=BVA; the one-cent-above-limit scenario is a boundary probe (it is the first value outside the valid partition), so kind must be boundary to match BVA. EP covers a nominal valid amount well inside the range with kind=positive. NEG covers the insufficient-funds rejection (409) with kind=negative. STC covers the state transition from unverified to step-up-verified to approved for amounts exceeding the threshold with kind=positive. DT covers all combined rules with kind=boundary.

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_nominal_valid_amount
preconditions: Customer account exists and is authenticated
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=500.00 and valid ACH destination
trace: R-FIN-029: nominal amount well within $10,000 limit
EP: representative value from valid partition [0.01, 10000.00]
tests.executable:
def test_ach_transfer_nominal_valid_amount(ach_client, verified_ach_account):
    payload = {
        "source_account_id": verified_ach_account["account_id"],
        "destination_routing": verified_ach_account["routing"],
        "destination_account": verified_ach_account["external_account"],
        "amount": "500.00",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = ach_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert "batch_id" in body
    assert body["status"] == "queued"
    assert confirm_notification_queued(body["batch_id"])

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_at_exact_upper_boundary
preconditions: Customer account exists and is authenticated
trace: R-FIN-029: transfers UP TO $10,000 are permitted without step-up
tests.executable:
def test_ach_transfer_at_exact_upper_boundary(ach_client, verified_ach_account):
    payload = {
        "source_account_id": verified_ach_account["account_id"],
        "destination_routing": verified_ach_account["routing"],
        "destination_account": verified_ach_account["external_account"],
        "amount": "10000.00",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = ach_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "queued"
    assert "step_up_required" not in body or body.get("step_up_required") is False

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_one_cent_above_limit_without_stepup
preconditions: Customer account exists and is authenticated
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=10000.01 and no step-up token
expected: Response body error code equals STEP_UP_REQUIRED
trace: R-FIN-029: amounts above $10,000 require step-up; none provided
BVA: first value above upper boundary (10000.01); probes the boundary between valid and invalid partitions
tests.executable:
def test_ach_transfer_one_cent_above_limit_without_stepup(ach_client, verified_ach_account):
    payload = {
        "source_account_id": verified_ach_account["account_id"],
        "destination_routing": verified_ach_account["routing"],
        "destination_account": verified_ach_account["external_account"],
        "amount": "10000.01",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = ach_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["error"]["code"] == "STEP_UP_REQUIRED"
    assert no_batch_created(verified_ach_account["account_id"], "10000.01")

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_above_limit_with_stepup_succeeds
preconditions: Customer account exists and is authenticated
Step-up verification token obtained via stepup_token_fixture
actions: Acquire step-up token for the session
POST /transfers/ach with amount=15000.00 and step-up token in header
trace: R-FIN-029: step-up verification allows transfers above $10,000
STC: transition from requires-step-up state to approved state after step-up supplied
tests.executable:
def test_ach_transfer_above_limit_with_stepup_succeeds(ach_client, verified_ach_account, stepup_token):
    payload = {
        "source_account_id": verified_ach_account["account_id"],
        "destination_routing": verified_ach_account["routing"],
        "destination_account": verified_ach_account["external_account"],
        "amount": "15000.00",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    headers = {"X-Step-Up-Token": stepup_token}
    resp = ach_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "queued"
    assert "batch_id" in body

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_insufficient_funds
preconditions: Customer account exists and is authenticated
Account balance is $50.00 (insufficient for requested amount)
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=500.00 against account with only $50.00 balance
expected: Response body error code equals INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS
trace: R-FIN-029: error pathway - 409 on insufficient funds
NEG: invalid precondition (insufficient funds) triggers expected error response
tests.executable:
def test_ach_transfer_insufficient_funds(ach_client, low_balance_account):
    payload = {
        "source_account_id": low_balance_account["account_id"],
        "destination_routing": low_balance_account["routing"],
        "destination_account": low_balance_account["external_account"],
        "amount": "500.00",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = ach_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 409
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["error"]["code"] == "INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS"
    assert no_batch_created(low_balance_account["account_id"], "500.00")

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_decision_table_all_rules
preconditions: Customer account exists and is authenticated
Parametrized fixture supplies (amount, step_up, sufficient_funds, expected_status, expected_code)
actions: For each decision-table row: POST /transfers/ach with given amount, optional step-up token, and account balance state
trace: R-FIN-029: all combinations of amount threshold, step-up presence, and fund availability
DT: exhaustive rule coverage for the two-condition decision table (amount vs limit, funds sufficiency)
